aliases: Hojicha Yuzu Tart with Vanilla Chantilly

english: Hojicha Yuzu Tart with Vanilla Chantilly

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a generic tart class (FOODON_00002473). No specific hojicha yuzu tart entry exists; closest_parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0FKP covers tarts and flans as a category. No specific hojicha or yuzu tart code exists; closest_parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT concept for hojicha yuzu tart or analogous pastry tart found in the clinical terminology.

main_ingredients: financier base (almond flour, butter, egg whites, sugar)

serving.natural_language_portion: One hojicha yuzu tart piece, approximately 80 g, as served at Kazo Premium Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Fruit tart, with custard filling (SR Legacy / Foundation Foods composite); supplemented with USDA entries for almond financier, whipped cream, and hojicha tea components
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service, FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry for hojicha yuzu tart exists in HPB Singapore Foodo, USDA FoodData Central, or FAO/INFOODS SEA. Nutrient values are estimated by component-weighted averaging of: (1) USDA FDC #172764 — Tart shell, pastry, baked (butter shortcrust); (2) USDA FDC #169655 — Lemon curd (used as yuzu curd proxy, similar sugar/fat/egg profile); (3) USDA FDC #174263 — Cake, almond (financier proxy); (4) USDA FDC #170859 — Whipped cream (vanilla chantilly proxy); (5) USDA FDC #171917 — Tea, green, brewed (hojicha crémeux tea fraction). Approximate component proportions: tart shell 30%, yuzu curd 25%, hojicha crémeux 20%, financier base 15%, vanilla chantilly 10%. Hojicha contributes negligible macronutrients at the crémeux fraction level. Values represent a reasonable central estimate; actual restaurant product may vary.

gi_source.title: Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281-2283. — Pastry / shortcrust tart shell analogue; supplemented by University of Sydney GI Database entry for shortcrust pastry and custard tart.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database; Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for hojicha yuzu tart. GI estimated at 55 (low) based on: (1) University of Sydney GI Database — custard tart / pastry tart entries cluster around GI 50–60; (2) Atkinson et al. 2008 — shortcrust pastry GI ~55; (3) yuzu curd and hojicha crémeux are high-fat, moderate-sugar components that moderate glycemic response relative to plain pastry. Serving size 80 g, carbohydrate per serving ~32 g (40 g/100g × 0.80), GL = 55 × 32 / 100 = 17.6. Classified low GI (GI 55 falls at the upper boundary of the low GI category).

hypertension.key_factor: Moderate sodium content (~92 mg per 80 g serving) and high saturated fat
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit this tart to occasional consumption, as the combined sodium (~92 mg/serving) and saturated fat (~8.6 g/serving) may contribute to cardiovascular strain; portion control to one piece and pairing with low-sodium meals is advised.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High sugar content (~17.6 g per serving), low glycemic index (GI 55), and medium glycemic load (GL 17.6 per serving)
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should treat this tart as an occasional indulgence only; despite its low GI (55), the medium glycemic load of 17.6 per 80 g serving and approximately 32 g of carbohydrates will still produce a meaningful postprandial glucose rise requiring careful carbohydrate budgeting within the daily meal plan due to its high sugar content.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High saturated fat (~10.8 g/100g; ~8.6 g per serving) from butter, cream, and egg yolks
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals managing cardiovascular disease should limit this tart to rare occasions, as the high saturated fat content (~8.6 g per 80 g serving, approximately 43% of the recommended daily limit) from butter pastry, crémeux, and chantilly cream may adversely affect LDL cholesterol levels.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Hojicha Yuzu Tart is a contemporary Japanese-inspired patisserie dessert sold in Singapore, notably at Kazo Premium (Funan). It consists of four distinct components: a yuzu curd layer providing bright, citrusy tartness; a hojicha crémeux — a silky, roasted-green-tea-infused custard cream with earthy, smoky-sweet notes characteristic of hojicha (a Japanese roasted green tea made from stems and leaves of Camellia sinensis); a vanilla chantilly cream topping that adds lightness and floral sweetness; and a financier base — a classic French almond cake made with beurre noisette (brown butter), almond flour, egg whites, and sugar, providing a moist, nutty foundation — all nestled in a buttery shortcrust pastry tart shell. The flavour profile balances the astringent earthiness of hojicha against the sharp citrus brightness of yuzu, softened by rich dairy cream and sweet almond cake. Texturally, the tart offers a crisp pastry shell, a dense financier layer, smooth crémeux, and airy chantilly. Nutritionally, one 80 g piece provides approximately 272 kcal, 32 g carbohydrates, 14.8 g fat (8.6 g saturated), and 4.2 g protein. The low glycemic index (GI ~55) but medium glycemic load of ~17.6 per serving make it a moderate glycemic concern. The high saturated fat and sugar content make it a high-concern food for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes and cardiovascular disease. It is vegetarian but contains gluten, dairy, eggs, and tree nuts (almonds), making it unsuitable for those with these allergies.

confidence_reason: No exact entry for this specific restaurant product exists in any approved primary source (HPB Singapore Foodo, USDA FoodData Central, FAO/INFOODS SEA). Nutrient values are estimated by component-weighted averaging of closely analogous USDA FoodData Central entries for shortcrust pastry tart shell, lemon curd, almond cake, whipped cream, and green tea. GI is estimated from University of Sydney GI Database analogues for custard tart and shortcrust pastry. Confidence is medium because the component analogy is well-grounded but actual product values may differ from the restaurant's specific recipe.
notes: 1. Serving size of 80 g is estimated based on typical single-piece patisserie tart dimensions (approximately 8–9 cm diameter, 2–3 cm height); actual weight at Kazo Premium Funan may differ. 2. Nutrient values are component-weighted estimates from USDA FoodData Central analogues; no direct laboratory analysis or HPB Foodo entry exists for this product. 3. GI of 55 is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database custard/pastry tart entries; no direct GI measurement for hojicha yuzu tart was found in peer-reviewed literature. 4. Halal status is unconfirmed; Kazo Premium's halal certification status was not verifiable from approved sources at time of processing. 5. monounsaturated_fat_g (5.4), polyunsaturated_fat_g (1.1), trans_fat_g (0.2), potassium_mg (95.0), calcium_mg (42.0), iron_mg (0.9), cholesterol_mg (95.0), vitamin_c_mg (3.5), vitamin_a_mcg_rae (85.0), vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg (0.06), vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg (0.09), vitamin_b3_niacin_mg (0.5), vitamin_b6_mg (0.04), vitamin_b12_mcg (0.2), folate_mcg_dfe (18.0), vitamin_d_mcg (0.4), vitamin_e_mg (0.8), phosphorus_mg (72.0), magnesium_mg (12.0), zinc_mg (0.4), selenium_mcg (5.0), and water_g (32.0) are all estimated from USDA FoodData Central component analogues; none are -1 sentinel values as reasonable estimates were derivable from the component analysis. 6. No -1 sentinel values are present in this record; all micronutrient fields carry estimated values derived from the component-weighted USDA source analysis described in nutrient_source.
